top_items.title: Watch live: Schumer, Democrats rail against GOP-led SAVE America Act
top_items.summary: Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.) and Sen. Alex Padilla (D-Calif.) will speak with reporters Wednesday morning to voice their opposition to the GOP-led Safeguard American Voter Eligibility (SAVE) America Act. The election reform bill, backed by President Trump, would require Americans to show proof of citizenship when registering to vote and to bring a…

top_items.title: Vice President Vance to meet with oil industry as White House plans more actions to address fuel prices
top_items.summary: Consumers are seeing fuel prices rapidly rise as the Iran war has triggered the biggest oil supply disruption in history.

top_items.title: Democrats brush off latest DHS counteroffer from White House: ‘Wholly inadequate’
top_items.summary: Democrats on Wednesday sharply criticized the White House’s latest counterproposal aimed at ending the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) shutdown, arguing that it falls short of sufficiently addressing their concerns about the administration’s immigration crackdown. The counterproposal, outlined in a letter by border czar Tom Homan and James Braid, the White House legislative affairs director,…

top_items.title: Furious MAGA allies lobby Trump to keep deporting migrants
top_items.summary: The new Mass Deportation Coalition is pushing the White House to continue with its deportation operation — just as the White House tells GOP candidates to back away from that message.

top_items.title: Padilla says he will ‘rework’ bill establishing Chávez National Park after abuse allegations
top_items.summary: Sen. Alex Padilla (D-Calif.) on Wednesday vowed to “rework” a bill he introduced last year proposing the creation of a national park dedicated to César Chávez, after a lengthy piece in The New York Times revealed numerous allegations of sexual abuse against the late civil rights leader. “We’re going to rework that piece of legislation,…

top_items.title: Crypto exchange Kraken freezes multibillion-dollar IPO plan due to difficult market conditions
top_items.summary: Kraken's parent company filed a draft S-1 registration statement with the SEC in November regarding the proposed initial public offering of its common stock.

top_items.title: Crypto Long & Short: When price stops working, yield starts mattering
top_items.summary: In this week’s Crypto Long & Short Newsletter, Ruchir Gupta writes on how we’re moving toward a true fixed-income market for crypto-native yield. Then, Clara García Prieto on bitcoin becoming mainstream collateral, but most are not prepared for its risks.

top_items.title: Traders can now bet on the S&P 500 around the clock without ever touching a traditional stock exchange
top_items.summary: S&P Dow Jones Indices licenses its flagship index for crypto trading on Hyperliquid, as perpetual futures gain traction beyond traditional markets.

top_items.title: AI, crypto industries dump millions into Illinois primaries to mixed results
top_items.summary: Illinois' early primary shows the limits of the AI and cryptocurrency industries' spending power on shaping Congress.

top_items.title: SEC approves Nasdaq's move to support tokenized securities trading
top_items.summary: The SEC’s approval lets Nasdaq test blockchain-based versions of stocks that trade and settle like traditional shares.

top_items.title: Even when they win it still ends in defeat - Spurs' season summed up
top_items.summary: Tottenham can take encouragement in their battle for Premier League survival despite a Champions League exit, writes Phil McNulty.

top_items.title: Meta is shutting down VR social platform Horizon Worlds in further pivot away from the metaverse
top_items.summary: Meta is scaling back its metaverse focus, cutting studios and head count at Reality Labs to prioritize artificial intelligence.

top_items.title: WNBA and players' union reach verbal agreement on landmark new CBA deal
top_items.summary: The verbal agreement marks the end of a prolonged and often contentious battle that threatened the start of the WNBA season.

top_items.title: Fetterman says Democrats governed by Trump Derangement Syndrome

John Fetterman (D-Pa.), who has been known to buck his party in recent years, said in a Wednesday podcast episode that the Democrats are governed by Trump Derangement Syndrome (TDS). “Who do you think leads the Democratic Party today?” The “All-in” podcast’s David Friedberg asked Fetterman in an interview posted Wednesday. “Oh, we don’t,…

top_items.title: IAEA chief skeptical Iran’s nuclear program can be eliminated with just military action
top_items.summary: The International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) Director General, Rafael Mariano Grossi, said that he does not believe that Iran’s nuclear program can be eliminated solely by military action.  “I would agree with that. Because this program is a very vast program. It’s a program that has been built throughout the years, decades of efforts, and…

top_items.title: Inflation dominates Powell's remarks, pressures stocks. Plus, big earnings tonight
top_items.summary: Every weekday, the Investing Club releases the Homestretch; an actionable afternoon update just in time for the last hour of trading.

top_items.title: Federal Reserve holds policy steady as Iran war adds to growth and inflation concerns
top_items.summary: Bitcoin remained sharply lower for the session following the expected decision by the U.S. central bank.

top_items.title: Fed to still cut rates this year, even as high oil prices spark an uptick in inflation: CNBC Fed Survey
top_items.summary: The 32 respondents, including fund managers, analysts and economists, see oil prices on average at $88 a barrel six months from now.

top_items.title: The era of cheap money is over as the Iran war creates a permanent 'inflation floor'
top_items.summary: The Iran war is creating a permanent inflation floor that could end the era of cheap money and expose the fragility of global energy markets.

top_items.title: Bitcoin quickly pulls back to $71,000 as Iran fears team up with poor U.S. inflation data

top_items.title: Oil nears $110 a barrel after gas field strike
top_items.summary: Iran's military warned it would take "decisive action" in response to the strike on its energy infrastructure.

top_items.title: Israel says it has killed Iran’s intelligence minister in third assassination in two days
top_items.summary: Israel's Defense Forces said Wednesday that Iran's intelligence minister, Esmail Khatib, had been killed in a "targeted strike" in Tehran.

top_items.title: Traffic is trickling through Strait of Hormuz: Who's moving and who's stranded
top_items.summary: Iran has scared off most ships from the Strait of Hormuz, leaving some ships to pass through, while most continued to wait outside the strait.

top_items.title: The Memo: Trump fumes as foreign leaders show new willingness to defy him
top_items.summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz has none of President Trump’s penchant for controversy or headline-grabbing language — but these days, he’s putting emphatic distance between Berlin and Washington all the same. Merz is not only declining to answer Trump’s call for help in reopening the vital Strait of Hormuz. He is being scathing of the president’s…

top_items.title: Iran threatens strikes on oil and gas targets after South Pars gas field hit
top_items.summary: The Iranian government threatened to strike oil and gas facilities in Qatar, Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates (UAE) on Wednesday, in response to an attack on its South Pars natural gas field. Iranian state media warned the country would target Saudi Arabia’s Samref Refiney and its Jubail Petrochemical Complex, as well as the…

top_items.title: Meta will pay Instagram, TikTok and YouTube creators with big followings to post on Facebook
top_items.summary: Meta is offering monthly guaranteed pay and boosted reach for creators to start posting on Facebook's Reels.

top_items.title: Hittler faces Zielinski as French town's election contest goes viral
top_items.summary: Charles Hittler, mayor of Arcis-sur-Aube, has always faced jokes about his name but now he faces a rival with another unlikely name.
